content.text: I'll start by reading the files that need to be modified.

content.content:
1→package search
     2→
     3→// DataSource describes the origin of one or more search fields.
     4→type DataSource struct {
     5→	// ID is a short, unique, kebab-case identifier (e.g. "nces-ipeds").
     6→	ID string
     7→	// Name is the full human-readable name of the data source.
     8→	Name string
     9→	// ShortName is a compact label suitable for UI badges or tooltips.
    10→	ShortName string
    11→	// Dataset is the top-level JSONB key or logical grouping that ties
    12→	// this source to columns in the search_base.dataset_merged view.
    13→	Dataset string
    14→	// URL points to the canonical homepage or documentation for the source.
    15→	URL string
    16→	// Description is a one-sentence summary of what the source provides.
    17→	Description string
    18→}
    19→
    20→// ── Source catalog ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    21→
    22→var (
    23→	SourceNCESIPEDS = DataSource{
    24→		ID:          "nces-ipeds",
    25→		Name:        "National Center for Education Statistics (IPEDS)",
    26→		ShortName:   "NCES",
    27→		Dataset:     "nces",
    28→		URL:         "https://nces.ed.gov/ipeds/",
    29→		Description: "Federal survey data covering academics, cost, outcomes, enrollment, and demographics.",
    30→	}
    31→
    32→	SourceUSNewsNational = DataSource{
    33→		ID:          "us-news-national",
    34→		Name:        "U.S. News & World Report — National Universities",
    35→		ShortName:   "US News",
    36→		Dataset:     "us_news____national_universities",
    37→		URL:         "https://www.usnews.com/best-colleges/rankings/national-universities",
    38→		Description: "Annual ranking of national universities by U.S. News & World Report.",
    39→	}
    40→
    41→	SourceUSNewsLiberalArts = DataSource{
    42→		ID:          "us-news-liberal-arts",
    43→		Name:        "U.S. News & World Report — Liberal Arts Colleges",
    44→		ShortName:   "US News",
    45→		Dataset:     "us_news____liberal_arts_colleges",
    46→		URL:         "https://www.usnews.com/best-colleges/rankings/national-liberal-arts-colleges",
    47→		Description: "Annual ranking of liberal arts colleges by U.S. News & World Report.",
    48→	}
    49→
    50→	SourceForbes = DataSource{
    51→		ID:          "forbes",
    52→		Name:        "Forbes America's Top Colleges",
    53→		ShortName:   "Forbes",
    54→		Dataset:     "forbes_____",
    55→		URL:         "https://www.forbes.com/top-colleges/",
    56→		Description: "Forbes ranking of America's top colleges.",
    57→	}
    58→
    59→	SourceWashingtonMonthly = DataSource{
    60→		ID:          "washington-monthly",
    61→		Name:        "Washington Monthly College Rankings",
    62→		ShortName:   "Wash Monthly",
    63→		Dataset:     "washington_monthly____national_universities",
    64→		URL:         "https://washingtonmonthly.com/rankings/",
    65→		Description: "Rankings emphasising social mobility, research, and public service.",
    66→	}
    67→
    68→	SourceNiche = DataSource{
    69→		ID:          "niche",
    70→		Name:        "Niche Best Colleges",
    71→		ShortName:   "Niche",
    72→		Dataset:     "niche____best_colleges",
    73→		URL:         "https://www.niche.com/colleges/",
    74→		Description: "Niche best colleges ranking based on academics, value, and student reviews.",
    75→	}
    76→
    77→	SourceMoney = DataSource{
    78→		ID:          "money",
    79→		Name:        "Money Magazine Best Colleges",
    80→		ShortName:   "Money",
    81→		Dataset:     "money_com_____",
    82→		URL:         "https://money.com/best-colleges/",
    83→		Description: "Money magazine college value score balancing quality, affordability, and outcomes.",
    84→	}
    85→
    86→	SourceNCAA = DataSource{
    87→		ID:          "ncaa",
    88→		Name:        "National Collegiate Athletic Association",
    89→		ShortName:   "NCAA",
    90→		Dataset:     "ncaa",
    91→		URL:         "https://www.ncaa.org/",
    92→		Description: "NCAA division and conference data for intercollegiate athletics.",
    93→	}
    94→
    95→	SourceFiveThirtyEight = DataSource{
    96→		ID:          "fivethirtyeight",
    97→		Name:        "FiveThirtyEight Partisan Lean",
    98→		ShortName:   "538",
    99→		Dataset:     "partisan_lean_538",
   100→		URL:         "https://fivethirtyeight.com/",
   101→		Description: "Congressional-district-level partisan lean scores.",
   102→	}
   103→
   104→	SourceKoppen = DataSource{
   105→		ID:          "koppen",
   106→		Name:        "Köppen Climate Classification",
   107→		ShortName:   "Köppen",
   108→		Dataset:     "koppen",
   109→		URL:         "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/K%C3%B6ppen_climate_classification",
   110→		Description: "Climate classification by temperature and precipitation patterns.",
   111→	}
   112→
   113→	SourceCommonApp = DataSource{
   114→		ID:          "common-app",
   115→		Name:        "College Application Platforms",
   116→		ShortName:   "Apps",
   117→		Dataset:     "applications",
   118→		URL:         "https://www.commonapp.org/",
   119→		Description: "Application platforms accepted by each institution (Common App, Coalition, etc.).",
   120→	}
   121→
   122→	SourceUSCensus = DataSource{
   123→		ID:          "us-census",
   124→		Name:        "U.S. Census Bureau",
   125→		ShortName:   "Census",
   126→		Dataset:     "metro_area",
   127→		URL:         "https://www.census.gov/",
   128→		Description: "Metropolitan area and locale classification data.",
   129→	}
   130→)
   131→
   132→// AllDataSources returns every registered data source.
   133→func AllDataSources() []DataSource {
   134→	return []DataSource{
   135→		SourceNCESIPEDS,
   136→		SourceUSNewsNational,
   137→		SourceUSNewsLiberalArts,
   138→		SourceForbes,
   139→		SourceWashingtonMonthly,
   140→		SourceNiche,
   141→		SourceMoney,
   142→		SourceNCAA,
   143→		SourceFiveThirtyEight,
   144→		SourceKoppen,
   145→		SourceCommonApp,
   146→		SourceUSCensus,
   147→	}
   148→}
   149→

1→package search
     2→
     3→import "sort"
     4→
     5→// Registry holds every registered search field, keyed by name.
     6→type Registry struct {
     7→	fields map[string]Field
     8→}
     9→
    10→// Lookup returns the Field for the given name, or false if unknown.
    11→func (r *Registry) Lookup(name string) (Field, bool) {
    12→	f, ok := r.fields[name]
    13→	return f, ok
    14→}
    15→
    16→// All returns every registered field in an unspecified order.
    17→func (r *Registry) All() []Field {
    18→	out := make([]Field, 0, len(r.fields))
    19→	for _, f := range r.fields {
    20→		out = append(out, f)
    21→	}
    22→	return out
    23→}
    24→
    25→// Descriptors returns metadata for every registered field. This is the
    26→// contract consumed by the future GET /api/search/fields handler.
    27→func (r *Registry) Descriptors() []FieldDescriptor {
    28→	out := make([]FieldDescriptor, 0, len(r.fields))
    29→	for _, f := range r.fields {
    30→		out = append(out, f.Descriptor())
    31→	}
    32→	return out
    33→}
    34→
    35→// OutputDescriptors returns metadata for fields that appear in search result
    36→// columns (OutputDefault != nil). The caller decides how to order them.
    37→func (r *Registry) OutputDescriptors() []FieldDescriptor {
    38→	out := make([]FieldDescriptor, 0, len(r.fields))
    39→	for _, f := range r.fields {
    40→		d := f.Descriptor()
    41→		if d.OutputDefault != nil {
    42→			out = append(out, d)
    43→		}
    44→	}
    45→	return out
    46→}
    47→
    48→// ExportableDescriptors returns metadata for fields that are exportable and
    49→// have an output representation. Results are ordered by theme (AllThemes order)
    50→// then by registration order within each theme.
    51→func (r *Registry) ExportableDescriptors() []FieldDescriptor {
    52→	// Build theme-index map for sorting.
    53→	themeIndex := make(map[Theme]int, len(AllThemes))
    54→	for i, t := range AllThemes {
    55→		themeIndex[t] = i
    56→	}
    57→
    58→	// Collect qualifying fields preserving registration order.
    59→	type indexedDesc struct {
    60→		regOrder int
    61→		desc     FieldDescriptor
    62→	}
    63→	var items []indexedDesc
    64→	for i, f := range allFields() {
    65→		d := f.Descriptor()
    66→		if d.Exportable != nil && *d.Exportable && d.OutputDefault != nil {
    67→			items = append(items, indexedDesc{regOrder: i, desc: d})
    68→		}
    69→	}
    70→
    71→	// Sort by theme order, stable within theme (registration order).
    72→	sort.SliceStable(items, func(i, j int) bool {
    73→		ti := themeIndex[items[i].desc.Theme]
    74→		tj := themeIndex[items[j].desc.Theme]
    75→		if ti != tj {
    76→			return ti < tj
    77→		}
    78→		return items[i].regOrder < items[j].regOrder
    79→	})
    80→
    81→	out := make([]FieldDescriptor, len(items))
    82→	for i, item := range items {
    83→		out[i] = item.desc
    84→	}
    85→	return out
    86→}
    87→
    88→// NewRegistry constructs and returns a fully populated Registry.
    89→func NewRegistry() *Registry {
    90→	r := &Registry{fields: make(map[string]Field)}
    91→	for _, f := range allFields() {
    92→		r.fields[f.Name()] = f
    93→	}
    94→	return r
    95→}
    96→
    97→// NewRegistryFromFields builds a Registry from an explicit list of fields.
    98→// Intended for testing scenarios that need custom field configurations.
    99→func NewRegistryFromFields(fields []Field) *Registry {
   100→	r := &Registry{fields: make(map[string]Field, len(fields))}
   101→	for _, f := range fields {
   102→		r.fields[f.Name()] = f
   103→	}
   104→	return r
   105→}
   106→
   107→// allFields returns the complete list of search fields.
   108→// Every field must set visibility, searchability, and exportable explicitly — no defaults.
   109→func allFields() []Field {
   110→	return []Field{
   111→		// ── Core ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   112→		&FullTextSearchField{baseField: baseField{
   113→			name: "name", label: "Institution Name",
   114→			description:   "Full-text search on institution name, city, and state",
   115→			kind:          KindFullText,
   116→			theme:         ThemeCore,
   117→			source:        FieldSource{Kind: SourceTSVector, Column: "name_location_search_vector"},
   118→			visibility:    VisibilityVisible,
   119→			searchability: SearchabilitySearchable,
   120→			exportable:    boolPtr(true),
   121→			outputDefault: boolPtr(true),
   122→			sortable:      true,
   123→			dataSourceID:  SourceNCESIPEDS.ID,
   124→		}},
   125→
   126→		// unit_id: hidden from UI but available as an output field for API
   127→		// consumers (e.g. when building deep-links or cross-referencing data).
   128→		&ExactStringField{
   129→			baseField: baseField{
   130→				name: "unit_id", label: "Unit ID",
   131→				description:   "Exact match on the NCES institution unit ID",
   132→				kind:          KindExactString,
   133→				theme:         ThemeCore,
   134→				source:        FieldSource{Kind: SourceColumn, Column: "unit_id"},
   135→				visibility:    VisibilityHidden,
   136→				searchability: SearchabilitySearchable,
   137→				exportable:    boolPtr(true),
   138→				outputDefault: boolPtr(false),
   139→				sortable:      false,
   140→				dataSourceID:  SourceNCESIPEDS.ID,
   141→			},
   142→			CaseSensitive: true,
   143→		},
   144→
   145→		// ── Location ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   146→		&SubstringField{baseField: baseField{
   147→			name: "city", label: "City",
   148→			description:   "City where the institution is located",
   149→			kind:          KindSubstring,
   150→			theme:         ThemeLocation,
   151→			source:        FieldSource{Kind: SourceColumn, Column: "city"},
   152→			visibility:    VisibilityHidden,
   153→			searchability: SearchabilityNotSearchable,
   154→			exportable:    boolPtr(true),
   155→			outputDefault: boolPtr(true),
   156→			sortable:      true,
   157→			dataSourceID:  SourceNCESIPEDS.ID,
   158→		}},
   159→
   160→		&MultiColumnEnumField{
   161→			baseField: baseField{
   162→				name: "state", label: "State",
   163→				description:   "US state where the institution is located",
   164→				kind:          KindMultiEnum,
   165→				theme:         ThemeLocation,
   166→				source:        FieldSource{Kind: SourceColumn, Column: "state"},
   167→				visibility:    VisibilityVisible,
   168→				searchability: SearchabilitySearchable,
   169→				exportable:    boolPtr(true),
   170→				outputDefault: boolPtr(true),
   171→				sortable:      true,
   172→				dataSourceID:  SourceNCESIPEDS.ID,
   173→			},
   174→			Options: allStateOptions(),
   175→		},
   176→
   177→		// outputDefault=nil: raw coordinates are not a useful result column.
   178→		// exportable=false: would enable bulk geocoding of all institutions.
   179→		&GeoField{
   180→			baseField: baseField{
   181→				name: "location", label: "Location",
   182→				description:   "Geographic proximity filter (radius or bounding box)",
   183→				kind:          KindGeo,
   184→				theme:         ThemeLocation,
   185→				source:        FieldSource{Kind: SourceGeo, Column: "coordinates"},
   186→				visibility:    VisibilityHidden,
   187→				searchability: SearchabilitySearchable,
   188→				exportable:    boolPtr(false),
   189→				outputDefault: nil,
   190→				sortable:      false,
   191→				dataSourceID:  SourceNCESIPEDS.ID,
   192→			},
   193→			MaxRadiusKm: 500,
   194→		},
   195→
   196→		&SingleEnumField{
   197→			baseField: baseField{
   198→				name: "locale", label: "Locale",
   199→				description:   "IPEDS locale code describing the campus setting",
   200→				kind:          KindSingleEnum,
   201→				theme:         ThemeLocation,
   202→				source:        FieldSource{Kind: SourceJSONBPath, JSONBPath: []string{"nces", "locale"}, ValueType: JSONBText},
   203→				visibility:    VisibilityVisible,
   204→				searchability: SearchabilitySearchable,
   205→				exportable:    boolPtr(true),
   206→				outputDefault: boolPtr(false),
   207→				sortable:      false,
   208→				outputKey:     "locationDetails.locale",
   209→				dataSourceID:  SourceUSCensus.ID,
   210→			},
   211→			Options: []EnumOption{
   212→				{Value: "11", Label: "City: Large"},
   213→				{Value: "12", Label: "City: Midsize"},
   214→				{Value: "13", Label: "City: Small"},
   215→				{Value: "21", Label: "Suburb: Large"},
   216→				{Value: "22", Label: "Suburb: Midsize"},
   217→				{Value: "23", Label: "Suburb: Small"},
   218→				{Value: "31", Label: "Town: Fringe"},
   219→				{Value: "32", Label: "Town: Distant"},
   220→				{Value: "33", Label: "Town: Remote"},
   221→				{Value: "41", Label: "Rural: Fringe"},
   222→				{Value: "42", Label: "Rural: Distant"},
   223→				{Value: "43", Label: "Rural: Remote"},
   224→			},
   225→		},
   226→
   227→		&SingleEnumField{
   228→			baseField: baseField{
   229→				name: "region", label: "Region",
   230→				description:   "NCES geographic region",
   231→				kind:          KindSingleEnum,
   232→				theme:         ThemeLocation,
   233→				source:        FieldSource{Kind: SourceJSONBPath, JSONBPath: []string{"nces", "region_id"}, ValueType: JSONBText},
   234→				visibility:    VisibilityVisible,
   235→				searchability: SearchabilitySearchable,
   236→				exportable:    boolPtr(true),
   237→				outputDefault: boolPtr(false),
   238→				sortable:      false,
   239→				outputKey:     "locationDetails.region",
   240→				dataSourceID:  SourceNCESIPEDS.ID,
   241→			},
   242→			Options: []EnumOption{
   243→				{Value: "1", Label: "New England"},
   244→				{Value: "2", Label: "Mid East"},
   245→				{Value: "3", Label: "Great Lakes"},
   246→				{Value: "4", Label: "Plains"},
   247→				{Value: "5", Label: "Southeast"},
   248→				{Value: "6", Label: "Southwest"},
   249→				{Value: "7", Label: "Rocky Mountains"},
   250→				{Value: "8", Label: "Far West"},
   251→				{Value: "9", Label: "Outlying Areas"},
   252→			},
   253→		},
   254→
   255→		&ExactStringField{
   256→			baseField: baseField{
   257→				name: "metro_area", label: "Metro Area",
   258→				description:   "Metropolitan area name (case-insensitive match)",
   259→				kind:          KindExactString,
   260→				theme:         ThemeLocation,
   261→				source:        FieldSource{Kind: SourceJSONBPath, JSONBPath: []string{"metro_area", "name"}, ValueType: JSONBText},
   262→				visibility:    VisibilityVisible,
   263→				searchability: SearchabilityNotSearchable,
   264→				exportable:    boolPtr(true),
   265→				outputDefault: boolPtr(false),
   266→				sortable:      false,
   267→				outputKey:     "locationDetails.metroArea",
   268→				dataSourceID:  SourceUSCensus.ID,
   269→			},
   270→			CaseSensitive: false,
   271→		},
   272→
   273→		&NumericRangeField{
   274→			baseField: baseField{
   275→				name: "metro_distance", label: "Metro Distance",
   276→				description:   "Distance from the institution to the nearest metro area centre",
   277→				kind:          KindNumericRange,
   278→				theme:         ThemeLocation,
   279→				source:        FieldSource{Kind: SourceJSONBPath, JSONBPath: []string{"metro_area", "distance"}, ValueType: JSONBNumeric},
   280→				visibility:    VisibilityVisible,
   281→				searchability: SearchabilityNotSearchable,
   282→				exportable:    boolPtr(true),
   283→				outputDefault: boolPtr(false),
   284→				sortable:      true,
   285→				outputKey:     "locationDetails.metroDistance",
   286→				dataSourceID:  SourceUSCensus.ID,
   287→			},
   288→			Units: "miles",
   289→		},
   290→
   291→		// ── Academics ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   292→		&SingleEnumField{
   293→			baseField: baseField{
   294→				name: "institution_type", label: "Institution Type",
   295→				description:   "Control / ownership type of the institution",
   296→				kind:          KindSingleEnum,
   297→				theme:         ThemeAcademics,
   298→				source:        FieldSource{Kind: SourceJSONBPath, JSONBPath: []string{"nces", "ownership"}, ValueType: JSONBText},
   299→				visibility:    VisibilityVisible,
   300→				searchability: SearchabilitySearchable,
   301→				exportable:    boolPtr(true),
   302→				outputDefault: boolPtr(true),
   303→				sortable:      false,
   304→				outputKey:     "academics.institutionType",
   305→				dataSourceID:  SourceNCESIPEDS.ID,
   306→			},
   307→			Options: []EnumOption{
   308→				{Value: "1", Label: "Public"},
   309→				{Value: "2", Label: "Private nonprofit"},
   310→				{Value: "3", Label: "Private for-profit"},
   311→			},
   312→		},
   313→
   314→		&SingleEnumField{
   315→			baseField: baseField{
   316→				name: "highest_degree", label: "Highest Degree Offered",
   317→				description:   "Highest degree awarded by the institution",
   318→				kind:          KindSingleEnum,
   319→				theme:         ThemeAcademics,
   320→				source:        FieldSource{Kind: SourceJSONBPath, JSONBPath: []string{"nces", "degrees_awarded_highest"}, ValueType: JSONBText},
   321→				visibility:    VisibilityVisible,
   322→				searchability: SearchabilitySearchable,
   323→				exportable:    boolPtr(true),
   324→				outputDefault: boolPtr(false),
   325→				sortable:      false,
   326→				outputKey:     "academics.highestDegree",
   327→				dataSourceID:  SourceNCESIPEDS.ID,
   328→			},
   329→			Options: []EnumOption{
   330→				{Value: "1", Label: "Certificate"},
   331→				{Value: "2", Label: "Associate's"},
   332→				{Value: "3", Label: "Bachelor's"},
   333→				{Value: "4", Label: "Master's"},
   334→				{Value: "5", Label: "Doctorate"},
   335→			},
   336→		},
   337→
   338→		&NumericRangeField{
   339→			baseField: baseField{
   340→				name: "enrollment", label: "Total Enrollment",
   341→				description:   "Total undergraduate enrollment",
   342→				kind:          KindNumericRange,
   343→				theme:         ThemeAcademics,
   344→				source:        FieldSource{Kind: SourceJSONBPath, JSONBPath: []string{"nces", "size"}, ValueType: JSONBInteger},
   345→				visibility:    VisibilityVisible,
   346→				searchability: SearchabilitySearchable,
   347→				exportable:    boolPtr(true),
   348→				outputDefault: boolPtr(true),
   349→				sortable:      true,
   350→				outputKey:     "academics.enrollment",
   351→				dataSourceID:  SourceNCESIPEDS.ID,
   352→			},
   353→			Units: "students",
   354→		},
   355→
   356→		&NumericRangeField{
   357→			baseField: baseField{
   358→				name: "grad_enrollment", label: "Graduate Enrollment",
   359→				description:   "Total graduate student enrollment",
   360→				kind:          KindNumericRange,
   361→				theme:         ThemeAcademics,
   362→				source:        FieldSource{Kind: SourceJSONBPath, JSONBPath: []string{"nces", "grad_students"}, ValueType: JSONBInteger},
   363→				visibility:    VisibilityHidden,
   364→				searchability: SearchabilitySearchable,
   365→				exportable:    boolPtr(true),
   366→				outputDefault: boolPtr(false),
   367→				sortable:      true,
   368→				outputKey:     "academics.gradEnrollment",
   369→				dataSourceID:  SourceNCESIPEDS.ID,
   370→			},
   371→			Units: "students",
   372→		},
   373→
   374→		&NumericRangeField{
   375→			baseField: baseField{
   376→				name: "student_faculty_ratio", label: "Student-Faculty Ratio",
   377→				description:   "Student-to-faculty ratio",
   378→				kind:          KindNumericRange,
   379→				theme:         ThemeAcademics,
   380→				source:        FieldSource{Kind: SourceJSONBPath, JSONBPath: []string{"nces", "demographics_student_faculty_ratio"}, ValueType: JSONBInteger},
   381→				visibility:    VisibilityVisible,
   382→				searchability: SearchabilitySearchable,
   383→				exportable:    boolPtr(true),
   384→				outputDefault: boolPtr(false),
   385→				sortable:      true,
   386→				outputKey:     "academics.studentFacultyRatio",
   387→				dataSourceID:  SourceNCESIPEDS.ID,
   388→			},
   389→		},
   390→

I need both the Edit and Bash tools to complete this task, but both have been denied. Let me explain what I need to do.

To complete issue kapow-j2l.4, I need to make the following file edits and run commands, but both the Edit tool (for modifying files) and the Bash tool (for running `sed`, `make api-generate`, `make web-schema-sync`, `make test-standard`, and `git commit`) have been denied.

Here is what needs to happen:

**File: `/home/ketan/project/kapow/.claude/worktrees/agent-a408a120/api/internal/search/datasource.go`**
1. Line 113: Rename `SourceCommonApp` to `SourceApplicationPlatforms`
2. Line 114: Change ID from `"common-app"` to `"application-platforms"`
3. Line 115: Change Name to `"Application Platforms"`
4. Line 118: Change URL to `""`
5. Line 119: Change Description to `"Application platforms accepted by each institution."`
6. Line 145: Change `SourceCommonApp,` to `SourceApplicationPlatforms,`

**File: `/home/ketan/project/kapow/.claude/worktrees/agent-a408a120/api/internal/search/registry.go`**
1. Line 838: Change `SourceCommonApp.ID` to `SourceApplicationPlatforms.ID`
2. Lines 840-844: Replace the Options list -- remove `{Value: "Universal College Application", Label: "Universal College Application"}` and add 11 new entries:
   - `{Value: "CBCA", Label: "Common Black College Application"}`
   - `{Value: "QuestBridge", Label: "QuestBridge"}`
   - `{Value: "ApplyTexas", Label: "ApplyTexas"}`
   - `{Value: "ApplySUNY", Label: "Apply SUNY"}`
   - `{Value: "UCApplication", Label: "UC Application"}`
   - `{Value: "CalStateApply", Label: "Cal State Apply"}`
   - `{Value: "UWSystem", Label: "Universities of Wisconsin"}`
   - `{Value: "ApplyIdaho", Label: "Apply Idaho"}`
   - `{Value: "GAFutures", Label: "GAfutures"}`
   - `{Value: "CFNC", Label: "NC College Connect"}`
   - `{Value: "FloridaSUS", Label: "Florida SUS"}`

**Then run in order:**
1. `make api-generate`
2. `make web-schema-sync`
3. `make test-standard`
4. `git commit` with a descriptive message (without merging to main)
